svg.text: Dual-window tradeoff paths: some middle lanes are real, some are not

text.tspan: The old sidecar compared two exact duals: the calm canonical dual and the flattest constant-looking dual. This follow-up traces the
whole exact path between them. Because the dual constraint is linear, every convex mix is still exact, so the real question is how
quickly the noise bill rises once you start flattening the synthesis window.
The 75% overlap path buys a lot of flatness before
Quarter-hop is the strongest compromise lane in the
The 75% overlap path has a middle lane, but the 50%
Compact read: exactness survives everywhere, so the
midpoint rows only track how much flatness the path
buys before noise and energy climb.
Best middle lane: Blackman-Harris at 75% overlap. Worst
